Package org.apache.juneau.rest.client

Class RestLogEntry

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.rest.client.RestLogEntry
public final class RestLogEntry extends Object
Structured log entry produced after every RestRequest.run() invocation.

Passed to a RestLogger in the finally block of RestRequest.run(). When the transport fails before a response is received, getResponse() is null and getError() is non-null.

Beta — API subject to change: This type is part of the next-generation REST client and HTTP stack (org.apache.juneau.marshall.ng.*). It is not API-frozen: binary- and source-incompatible changes may appear in the next major Juneau release (and possibly earlier).

See Also:
  • juneau-ng REST client
Since:
9.2.1

    Formats the entry using the default template: "METHOD URI -> STATUS REASON (ELAPSEDms)".

    Formats the entry using a named-placeholder template.

    Returns the elapsed time for the request (from before onInit to after the response is received).

    Returns the error that occurred, or null on success.

    Returns the log level computed by the configured RestLogLevelResolver.

    Returns the request that triggered this log entry.

    Returns true if the response contains a header with the given name (case-insensitive).

    Returns true if RestRequest.debug() was called on the originating request.

    Returns true if the call resulted in an error (transport error or HTTP status ≥ 400).

  • Method Details

    • getRequest

      public RestRequest getRequest()
      Returns the request that triggered this log entry.
      Returns:
      The request. Never null.

    • getResponse

      public RestResponse getResponse()
      Returns the response received, or null if the transport failed before a response was received.
      Returns:
      The response, or null.

    • getError

      public Throwable getError()
      Returns the error that occurred, or null on success.
      Returns:
      The error, or null.

    • getElapsed

      public Duration getElapsed()
      Returns the elapsed time for the request (from before onInit to after the response is received).
      Returns:
      The elapsed time. Never null.

    • isDebug

      public boolean isDebug()
      Returns true if RestRequest.debug() was called on the originating request.
      Returns:
      true if this is a debug request.

    • getLevel

      public System.Logger.Level getLevel()
      Returns the log level computed by the configured RestLogLevelResolver.
      Returns:
      The log level. Never null.

    • isError

      public boolean isError()
      Returns true if the call resulted in an error (transport error or HTTP status ≥ 400).
      Returns:
      true if this entry represents an error condition.

    • getStatusCode

      public int getStatusCode()
      Returns the HTTP status code, or 0 if no response was received.
      Returns:
      The status code.

    • hasResponseHeader

      public boolean hasResponseHeader(String name)
      Returns true if the response contains a header with the given name (case-insensitive).
      Parameters:
      name - The header name. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      true if the header is present.

    • format

      public String format()
      Formats the entry using the default template: "METHOD URI -> STATUS REASON (ELAPSEDms)".
      Returns:
      A formatted string. Never null.

    • format

      public String format(String template)
      Formats the entry using a named-placeholder template.

      Supported placeholders:

      • {method} — HTTP method (e.g. GET)
      • {uri} — Full request URI
      • {status} — Response status code, or 0 if no response
      • {reason} — Response reason phrase, or empty string
      • {elapsed} — Elapsed time (e.g. 42ms)
      • {error} — Error message, or empty string if no error
      Parameters:
      template - The template string. Must not be null.
      Returns:
      A formatted string. Never null.

    • toString

      public String toString()
      Overrides:
      toString in class Object

    • builder

      public static RestLogEntry.Builder builder()
      Creates a new builder.
      Returns:
      A new builder. Never null.
